commit.message:
fix: offline panel persists across tabs + shows 0 spots

3 bugs fixed:
1. Panel stayed open when switching tabs (Social, Voyage) — now
   auto-closes on tab change via changeTab()
2. Spot counts showed 0 because spotLoader is stubbed — now counts
   community spots from Firestore state by country code
3. Title changed from "Spots hors-ligne" to "Données hors-ligne"

i18n updated in FR/EN/ES/DE.

commit.message:
feat: GPS trust system with confirmation dialog and validation gating

Complete GPS verification system for spot validations:

1. "Disponible" (quickValidateSpot): checks GPS proximity, shows
   confirmation dialog if not nearby, user can confirm manually
2. "Mon expérience" (openTestSpot): GPS check before opening form,
   option to choose different date if GPS fails
3. Spot creation: GPS auto-check when experience date = today
4. Trust score: tracks GPS vs non-GPS validation ratio per user.
   Minimum 1/3 (33%) GPS validations required (after 3 total).
   Below threshold: validations silently not sent to Firebase.
5. i18n: 7 new keys in FR/EN/ES/DE

New file: src/services/gpsTrust.js (dialog, trust counters, GPS wrapper)

commit.message:
feat: fine-tune DOGEUSD, LINKUSD, ADAUSD — all 3 now cost-viable

Parameter optimization via breakeven WR analysis (spread/TP ratio):

DOGEUSD (was marginal: breakeven=66.7%, WR=66.4% → -0.3pp):
  - TP/SL widened 1.5×ATR→3×ATR (45→90 pips 1H, 90→180 4H, 225→450 D)
  - Threshold raised 0.65→0.70
  - Result: WFO 10/10 profitable, WR 68.2%, PF 3.37
  - New breakeven=58.3%, margin=+9.9pp ✓ → promoted to stable

LINKUSD (was failing: realistic -16%, simplified model only 54% WR):
  - TP/SL widened 1.5×ATR→2×ATR (33→44 pips 1H, 66→88 4H, 165→220 D)
  - Threshold raised 0.65→0.70
  - Result: WFO 11/11 profitable, WR 68.3%, PF 2.69
  - New breakeven=55.7%, margin=+12.6pp ✓ → promoted to stable

ADAUSD (breakeven 53.7%, margin was only +11.8pp at 0.65):
  - Threshold raised 0.65→0.70 only (spread ratio fine: 7.4%)
  - Result: 11/11 WFO confirmed at 0.70, WR 67.5%, margin +13.8pp
  - Stays beta (simplified backtest model still fragile)

Production models retrained for DOGEUSD and LINKUSD (TP/SL label change).
US30 pending TWELVEDATA_API_KEY for data download.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: compact API responses for browse — 72% smaller payloads

Added ?compact=true parameter to /plugins and /search endpoints.
Returns only the fields needed for card display: slug, name,
manufacturer (name + slug only), category, subcategory, image_url,
year. Strips: id, formats, daws, os, aliases, tags, description,
website, manual_url, video_url, is_free, price_type, timestamps.

Full response: 22KB for 24 plugins
Compact response: ~6KB for 24 plugins (72% reduction)

Frontend browse page now uses compact=true for all list queries.
Detail pages still fetch full responses. Backward compatible —
existing API consumers get full responses by default.

New models: PluginCompactResponse, ManufacturerCompactResponse.
New query: build_compact_responses() — 1 query (manufacturers
only, no aliases) vs 3 queries for full responses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix valloc allocator to skip existing VMAs — gcc compiles on Alpine

alloc_vaddr_range was a simple bump allocator that didn't check for
existing VMAs. After set_heap_bottom placed the heap VMA in the
valloc region, subsequent mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS) calls got addresses
that overlapped the heap VMA, causing EINVAL from add_vm_area.

musl's malloc depends on mmap(MAP_ANONYMOUS) for large allocations.
When mmap failed, BusyBox sh printed "out of memory" and gcc's
subprocesses couldn't run.

Fix: alloc_vaddr_range now loops and skips over any conflicting VMAs,
advancing valloc_next past the end of the conflict. This handles
heap VMAs, ELF file header pages, and any other VMAs that were placed
in the valloc region during ELF loading.

Result: gcc -o /root/t /root/t.c compiles successfully (gcc=OK).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Gibbs IMS multi-standard compliance platform

Multi-tenant SaaS platform for ISO management system compliance.
Covers 14 standards (ISO 42001, 27001, 9001, 27701, and 10 more)
with 640 controls, 41 training modules, and 20 harmonized (HLS)
requirements.

Stack: Express + TypeScript + Prisma + PostgreSQL (backend),
Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind (frontend), Docker Compose.

Features:
- Schema-per-tenant PostgreSQL isolation
- AI system inventory, risk register, lifecycle management
- Incident management, human oversight, monitoring
- Training modules with completion tracking
- Internal audit and management review
- Governance role assignments
- Multi-standard control mapping with compliance dashboard
- JWT auth with forced password reset on first login
- Activity logging across all modules

commit.message:
fix: PUT /contas não deve sobrescrever saldo gerenciado por transações

O endpoint de edição de conta aceitava o campo saldo do cliente e
o sobrescrevia, causando perda de saldo correto calculado pelas
transações. O saldo agora é imutável via edição — só pode ser
alterado através de criação/remoção de transações.

Saldo da Conta Corrente (id=1) corrigido diretamente no banco para
2670 (valor calculado corretamente: 1000 inicial - 30 despesa +
1700 receita).

Initial commit: MariaDB → PostgreSQL CDC pipeline

- CloudFormation stack (full-cdc-pipeline.yaml): MSK cluster, Debezium
  MSK Connect connector, Lambda CDC consumer with psycopg2, Secrets
  Manager for both MariaDB and PostgreSQL credentials (Dynamic
  References + runtime fetch), scoped IAM policies, SQS DLQ with SSE,
  CloudWatch alarms, Lambda EventSourceMapping
- Bulk initial load script (initial_load.py): server-side streaming
  cursor, parallel workers (ThreadPoolExecutor), MariaDB→PostgreSQL DDL
  translation (--create-tables), consistent snapshot + binlog handoff,
  SSL/TLS CA bundle support (--ssl-ca)
- Full documentation (Readme.md): architecture, step-by-step migration
  guide, type mapping table, operational runbook, known limitations

commit.message:
fix: cita campos com espaços no CSV para evitar divisão de colunas no Excel

Valores contendo espaços (datas, nomes de conta/categoria multi-palavra,
cabeçalho "Saldo da Conta") eram interpretados pelo Excel como separadores
adicionais, dividindo o conteúdo em colunas extras. Agora escapeCsvBr
envolve em aspas qualquer valor com espaço.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(i18n): detect added and modified posts, not only added

--diff-filter=A missed Spanish posts that already existed in the repo
but had no .en.md sibling. Changed to --diff-filter=AM in both
shared/detect.py and the workflow. The _needs_translation guard
already prevents re-translating posts that have an .en.md.

commit.message:
Improve Sky Sports matching: validate score and home/away order

Replace team-name-only matching with scoreline parsing. Extracts the
"HomeTeam SCORE AwayTeam" segment between pipe delimiters, validates
that the score matches the fixture, and that home/away order is correct.
Fixes cases where Arsenal vs Chelsea and Chelsea vs Arsenal could get
the same video. Fixture index now uses (home, away) tuple instead of
frozenset. Result: 376/382 videos matched.

commit.message:
Trigger LAND->TRANSITION phase when 6 active tanks exist

Rather than waiting for all home island cities to be captured, the AI
now transitions to naval production (TRANSITION phase) as soon as it
has 6 active tanks. This seeds transport and fighter production earlier
while tanks continue capturing remaining home cities. Capturing a city
may cost a tank, slightly delaying the trigger, but each new city also
adds a production queue — accelerating the ramp dramatically.

commit.message:
Handle RTM_SETLINK in netlink + always ACK set operations

BusyBox ip link set uses RTM_SETLINK (17) which wasn't handled,
causing ip to hang waiting for a response. Also always send ACK
for RTM_NEWLINK/SETLINK/NEWADDR/NEWROUTE even without NLM_F_ACK
flag, since BusyBox's ip expects a response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: new spot tier system (ancien/récent/fiable/populaire)

- Grey = "Ancien": spot not validated or used in 2+ years
- Blue = "Récent": community spot with activity within last 2 years
- Green = "Fiable": 3+ tests AND 3+ validations AND recent (unchanged)
- Gold = "Populaire": 10+ uses (replaces ambassador certified)

A green (reliable) spot goes back to grey if inactive for 2+ years.
Gold is now based on usage count (10+), not ambassador status.
Legend and i18n updated in all 4 languages (FR/EN/ES/DE).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: overhaul neural network clear sequence and post-clear interactions

What?
- Replace white flash with dim overlay for clear transition.
- All neurons turn gold (hue 45) on clear with burst sparks and arcs.
- Post-clear: right-click connects neurons with electric signals for 3s.
- Post-clear: scroll changes all neuron/synapse hue through full spectrum.
- Post-clear: left-click hold causes progressive overload — fast electric signals, sparks, glow, and system death at 20s with flashlight mode.
- Yellow neurons fade simultaneously with regular neurons on clear.
- Synapse base alpha reduced for subtler post-clear look.

- 클리어 시퀀스를 딤 오버레이 방식으로 전면 재설계.
- 클리어 시 모든 뉴런이 골드로 변환, 버스트 스파크 및 아크 효과.
- 클리어 후: 우클릭으로 뉴런 연결(전기 신호 3초), 스크롤로 전체 색상 변경.
- 클리어 후: 왼클릭 홀드로 점진적 과부하 — 전기 폭주, 스파크, 발광, 20초 후 시스템 소멸.

feat(cli): auto-detect AI CLI providers (codex -> claude -> gemini -> opencode)

Add provider auto-detection that finds installed AI CLI tools and uses
them in priority order: codex (gpt-5.4) -> claude (opus) -> gemini
(gemini-3) -> opencode. No API key configuration needed.

- `swarmforge review providers` shows detected CLIs and active provider
- `swarmforge review create <url>` auto-selects best available provider
- `swarmforge review create <url> --provider claude` forces a specific one
- Each provider adapter handles CLI-specific invocation (exec, -p, SDK)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Internet Archive upload pipeline for images and manuals

MusicBrainz-style permanent hosting via archive.org:

scripts/archive_images.py:
- Downloads each plugin image from its current hotlinked URL
- Uploads to Internet Archive as plugindb-{slug}/{slug}.ext
- Updates seed.json image_url to point to archive.org/download/
- Skips already-uploaded images, handles failures gracefully
- Supports --limit, --delay, --dry-run, --skip-existing

scripts/archive_manuals.py:
- Same pattern but for PDF manuals only (skips generic support pages)
- Uploads as plugindb-manual-{slug}/{slug}-manual.pdf
- 50MB max per PDF

Both use IA's S3-compatible API (PUT to s3.us.archive.org).
Requires --access-key and --secret-key from archive.org/account/s3.php

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete ArgoBeat v2 engine redesign — Brain.fm-quality brainwave entrainment

Rewrites the entire audio engine from scratch with invisible entrainment,
mood-based UX, and procedural soundscape synthesis.

Engine (@argobeat/engine v2.0.0):
- 5 mood system (Focus, Deep Work, Relax, Meditate, Sleep) with psychoacoustically
  accurate Hz ranges and mood-soundscape affinity matrix
- Invisible 3-layer entrainment modulation (AM + spectral + stereo panning) applied
  to content — no audible tones, Brain.fm approach
- Session randomization: random Hz within mood range, weighted-random soundscape
  category, random variation selection, random drift phase
- 92 procedural soundscape variations across 9 categories (rain, ocean, forest,
  cafe, fire, space, stream, wind, thunder) — 10+ per category
- Prime-length noise buffers (29s, 31s, 37s) for 9.2-hour non-repeating cycles
- Dual-instance crossfading manager — seamless 12s transitions between variations
- Procedural reverb via ConvolverNode with synthetic impulse responses
- Accent event system (bird chirps, thunder, crickets, drips, clicks, chimes)
- Frequency drift LFO with per-session random phase offset

Web app (argobeat-web v2.0.0):
- Mood-based UI — no Hz values shown to users
- 5 mood cards with gradient colors and descriptions
- Soundscape preference selector (Auto or specific category)
- Mood-colored visualizer and progress ring
- Clean landing page with feature highlights
- Deleted all 10 placeholder mp3 files (30s each, unusable)
- 79KB JS bundle, 204KB total build output

Legacy v1 files moved to _legacy/ and excluded from compilation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: use experience date for spot validation/test timestamps

When creating a spot or submitting a validation, the user specifies
when they actually hitchhiked (experienceDate). Previously, lastValidated
and lastTested were always set to the submission timestamp (new Date()),
which was confusing when someone reported an experience from weeks ago.

Now:
- addSpot: lastValidated/lastTested = experienceDate (not submission date)
- addValidation: lastTested = experienceDate (not submission date)
- quickValidateSpot: keeps new Date() (confirming "right now")
- spotLiveData: prefers experienceDate over createdAt for live dates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Deduplicate clients in Contract Billing and main dashboard

Setup Timing sheet has duplicate rows for some clients (e.g., Juliana,
John Vasquez). Now deduplicates by client name (case-insensitive),
keeping the last row for each client. Applied to both billing/index.html
and index.html. Bumped cache version to v4 to force refetch.
